dialogueChunks.intro: The grief has turned to anger, {player_name}. And angry people make dangerous decisions.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A group from {location} has decided to avenge their dead by striking the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint}. They're not soldiers, they're farmers and craftworkers blinded by rage. {target_npc} tried to stop them but they shouted them down. If they march on that camp, they'll be slaughtered, and we'll have more graves to dig. I need you to gather {quest_item} to reinforce our defenses as a counter-argument: show them that protecting what we have is more honorable than a suicide charge. The {enemy_type} have been reinforcing too, as if they expect us to come.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we can't channel this grief into something constructive, {quest_stakes} will suffer even more loss.

playerResponses.text: Vengeance raids get people killed. I'll give them a better option.
Stopping a grieving mob from marching to their deaths. Delicate work.
Angry people with weapons don't listen to reason. Stay clear.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the loss keeps compounding. Another family is leaving {location}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Two more families packed up and left in the night. They said they can't raise children in a place where death comes so easily. {target_npc} is trying to hold things together, but every departure takes skills, labor, and hope with it. The {enemy_type} have noticed the weakening defenses and are probing closer {location_hint}. We need {quest_item} to repair the outer walls and show the remaining families that {location} is worth defending. If the settlement shrinks much more, it won't be viable: {quest_stakes} depends on having enough hands to work the land and stand watch.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're bleeding people as surely as if the {enemy_type} were still inside our walls. We need a reason to stay.

playerResponses.text: A settlement that fights to survive gives its people a reason to stay. I'll shore up the defenses.
Population collapse and monster raids. A grim combination. What's the plan?
If people are leaving, maybe they're right to. Not every settlement survives.

dialogueChunks.intro: Something unexpected happened, {player_name}. The grief brought something else to the surface.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: While searching for {quest_item} to complete {quest_focus}, {target_npc} found evidence that the raid which killed our people wasn't random. The {enemy_type} targeted specific buildings, specific stores, as if guided by someone who knew our layout. There might be a collaborator in {location}, someone who traded information about our defenses for personal safety. If that's true, the loss we suffered was a betrayal, not just a tragedy. We need you to investigate the {enemy_type} camp {location_hint} and find proof of who gave them our secrets.
dialogueChunks.outro: If someone sold out their neighbors to save themselves, the dead deserve justice and the living deserve the truth.

playerResponses.text: Betrayal that led to deaths is unforgivable. I'll find the proof.
A collaborator inside the settlement. That changes everything. I'm in.
Hunting a traitor in a grieving community could tear it apart entirely.
